assertions.evidence: 'The classic Santa Fe sufferfest' is punchy and direct. The description stays in plain, natural prose throughout with no stiff or promotional language.
All three stats are present: '9,121-foot summit' (elevation), 'roughly 1,900 feet' (gain), and 'about 6 miles out and back' (distance).
'Just about every local has done at least once' captures the iconic, rite-of-passage status of the route.
'Summer heat can be brutal on the exposed upper slopes, making spring and fall the far better seasons to tackle this one' is specific and actionable.
Starts with 'Atalaya Mountain is the classic Santa Fe sufferfest' — specific and characterful, not a generic opener.
